rationale.summary: This PR adds client-side URMA data-plane health tracking and failover so persistently unhealthy fast-path traffic can switch workers while preserving TCP fallback availability. The walkthrough shows the change is shaped as a runtime tracker/config layer, a client-worker failure signal layer, an asynchronous worker-switch layer, and focused validation/docs coverage. The gathered critique focuses on repository provenance noise, one async stale-callback test gap, disabled-mode hot-path overhead, validator coverage, and a minor production API surface issue.

keyPoints.point: The runtime implementation adds UrmaSuccessRateTracker with fixed-window success/failure accounting, minimum-sample gating, strict threshold comparison, duplicate switch suppression, failed-switch backoff, and reset behavior.
The client worker API records URMA Set/Get outcomes and invokes a data-plane failure callback only after an unhealthy settled window claims the switch gate.
The asynchronous switch path introduces URMA_DATA_PLANE_FAILURE and preserves current-worker availability for this reason so TCP fallback can continue if no better candidate is available.

Validation includes tracker unit coverage and system tests for Set/Get failure sampling, TCP fallback continuity, failed switch behavior, dynamic config updates, backoff reset, and local-worker recovery.

critique_ledger.title: Raw assistant transcript exposes internal reasoning and tool inputs
critique_ledger.rationale: The added conversation log commits assistant thinking content, model metadata, and tool inputs into the repository, which can disclose internal implementation reasoning or future sensitive command/config content if the pattern is kept.

critique_ledger.title: Generated conversation transcript is checked into the repo
critique_ledger.rationale: The hidden JSONL file is not product code, tests, or durable documentation, but can be treated as source-backed context even though it can drift from the real implementation.

critique_ledger.title: Stale URMA failover callback branch has no regression test
critique_ledger.rationale: No added system test forces a worker switch between callback submission and execution, so a regression that fails to finish the stale switch attempt could leave switchInProgress stuck and suppress later URMA failovers.

critique_ledger.title: Disabled URMA failover still resets atomics on every hot-path sample
critique_ledger.rationale: When the threshold is set to 0.0, every URMA Set/Get sample still enters RecordUrmaResult and calls ResetWindow, adding atomic stores and cache-line traffic on the foreground request path.

critique_ledger.title: New URMA failover flag validators lack negative coverage
critique_ledger.rationale: The added tests only use valid flag values, so regressions accepting invalid ratios, non-finite input, or min_sample_count=0 would not be caught.

critique_ledger.title: Test-only accessor is exposed on the production tracker interface
critique_ledger.rationale: GetCurrentSampleCountForTest is declared in the production header without a test guard, making a test helper part of the normal class surface.

reasons: no blocking risk band, adherence failure, or L4-pending cohort
